codeReview.summary: The handler wiring itself looks reasonable, but the feature does not fully satisfy its own validation contract because the new browser-resolution test suite never exercises the remaining env.BROWSER.launch() branch in resolveBrowser().

issues.description: VAL-BWIRE-004 requires all resolveBrowser() branches to be exercised, including the env.BROWSER.launch() path implemented in apps/browser-executor/src/executeRunHandler.ts:259-260. The new suite only covers the injected browser, direct BrowserLike binding, fetch-based Cloudflare binding via puppeteer.launch(env.BROWSER), and the misconfigured-worker case, so the launch()-based branch is still unverified and could regress unnoticed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture docs still do not spell out that the production Cloudflare Browser Rendering binding is a fetch-based object passed to puppeteer.launch(env.BROWSER), even though the worker had to consult external Cloudflare docs to confirm that shape.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows WebSearch/FetchUrl calls to developers.cloudflare.com for @cloudflare/puppeteer. The skill documents puppeteer.launch(env.BROWSER) at .factory/skills/cf-worker/SKILL.md:35 and :93, while .factory/library/architecture.md:43 and :50 only mention the BROWSER binding generically and that resolveBrowser() handles 4 paths.

summary: Fail: the implementation appears close, but the feature's new tests do not cover the existing env.BROWSER.launch() browser-resolution branch required by VAL-BWIRE-004, so the review cannot sign off on full feature coverage.
